rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Petitioner Western Progressive, Llc For Recovery Of Reasonable Attorneys Fees And Costs And Request For Discharge And Dismissal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities ; Declaration Of Katherine S. Walker In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 10, 2017, line 11. Notice Of Motion And Motion By Petitioner Western Progressive, Llc For Recovery Of Reasonable Attorneys Fees And Costs And Request For Discharge And Dismissal GRANTED. Petitioner shall recover its fees and costs and deposit $19,648.50 with the Court. Petitioner shall be discharged and dismissed after the funds are deposited with the Court. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ADA SORTO ET AL VS. WEDGEWOOD, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Appearance At Depositions And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 10, 2017, line 1. DEFENDANT EAGLE VISTA EQUITIES LLC, GREEN APPLE PROPERTIES III, LLC, WEDGEWOOD (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS "WEDGEWOOD, INC.") Notice Of Hearing On Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Appearance At Depositions And Request For Monetary Sanctions GRANTED, no opposition filed.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ADA SORTO ET AL VS. WEDGEWOOD, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Wedgewood (Erroneously Sued As "Wedgewood Inc.")'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 10, 2017, line 1. Defendant Wedgewood (Erroneously Sued As "Wedgewood Inc.")'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions, And Request For Monetary Sanctions GRANTED in part. Objections are stricken as responses have not been timely served. Plaintiff shall produce no later than April 17, 2017 further verified responses to any of defendant's discovery for which an answer was not provided based on objection. Request to Deem Admissions Admitted is denied as responses have been served before the hearing. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,000 to be paid to defendants' counsel within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Mr. Balderama and Mr. Cox to be present. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 10, 2017, line 2. DEFENDANT ARDEANOUS MAYE, LADREENA WALTON, ARDEANOUS MAYE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION continued to April 13, 2017 to be heard with other pending motion. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RAJAN RAGHAVAN ET AL VS. JENNIFER CREELMAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 10, 2017, line 3. DEFENDANT JENNIFER CREELMAN, ALFRED DRIGGS, IV's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT continued to May 10, 2017. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide a courtesy copy of the operative complaint to department 501 no later than April 21, 2017 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. Opposing counsel is ordered to provide a declaration addressing failure to engage in meet and confer efforts (See Declaration of Robert Maltz) no later than April 21, 2017. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: FIVE BROTHERS INVESTMENTS LLC VS. DORIS ELIZABETH BARRETT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses To Disco From Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 10, 2017, line 8. rulings.Case Title: DYNASTY PROPERTIES, LLC, VS. JEFFREY KWONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Defendant To Allow Property Inspection And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 10, 2017, line 9. PLAINTIFF DYNASTY PROPERTIES, LLC's, Motion To Compel Defendant To Allow Property Inspection And Request For Monetary Sanctions GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall make the Premises, as described in the Complaint, available for inspection on a mutually agreed date no later than April 21, 2017. If parties cannot agree on a date before April 12, 2017, Plaintiff to serve a Notice of Inspection for any date in accordance with the Discovery Act. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,060. = (501/REQ)
